chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, LARKANA C. P. No.D-969 of 2025 Date of Hearing ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 28.10.2025. 1. For orders on office objection. 2. For hearing of Main Case. Mr. Abdul Rehman A. Bhutto, advocate along with petitioner. Mr.Liaquat Ali Shar, Addl. A.G., along with Ghulam Mustafa Shaikh, Principal, BSAMS CMW School, Jacobabad and Munir Ahmed Soomro, Deputy DHO (RMNCH), Jacobabad. ------------------------ Respondents No.6 & 7 have filed statements/comments, which are taken on record. Learned Counsel for the petitioner also files Affidavit-in- Rejoinder against comments filed by respondent No.5. Learned Counsel for the petitioner submits that the petitioner fulfilling the requisite criteria applied for admission in Community Midwifery Training Course Batch 17 for the Academic Session 2025-27 and she appeared in the interviews held on 02.9.2025 and qualified in the test. Per learned Counsel, the petitioner possessing above percentage in educational qualification i.e. Matriculation with 67.53 percentage and Intermediate with 75.18 percentage was entitled and eligible to be given admission, but the respondents acting under pick and choose policy selected their blue eyed candidates on the basis of influence, while the petitioner has been deprived of such admission; otherwise, she is entitled to be given admission to above course purely on merit basis. Pursuant to notices, the respondents No.6 & 7 have appeared and have filed statement adopting the comments already filed by respondent No.5. The respondent No.5/District Health Officer, Jacobabad, who is said to be the chairman of admission selection committee, in his comments has mentioned that the total 122 candidates had applied for the above admission, out of which 117 candidates appear in the interview and only 30 candidates were selected on merit as per selection criteria.

Pursuant to notices, the respondents No.6 & 7 have appeared and have filed statement adopting the comments already filed by respondent No.5. The respondent No.5/District Health Officer, Jacobabad, who is said to be the chairman of admission selection committee, in his comments has mentioned that the total 122 candidates had applied for the above admission, out of which 117 candidates appear in the interview and only 30 candidates were selected on merit as per selection criteria. From perusal of the Attendance-Sheet of the Admission Committee for the admission of Community Midwives under Academic Session 2025-27 at Begum Saeeda Ahmed Mian Soomro Community Midwifery School, Jacobabad, dated 02.09.2025, it appears that 122 candidates had appeared, which negates the assertion of respondent No.5 made in his comments. When enquired, the respondents No.7 have not replied satisfactorily, while stating that it is a clerical mistake. In such circumstances, respondent No.5 is directed to appear before the Court in person on the next date and to explain the above anomalous position. Respondent No.7 submits that the classes of above course have been started in the BSAMS CMW School, Jacobabad since last about 11⁄2 month. Learned Counsel for the petitioner submits that since she has been deprived from admission in Community Midwifery Training Course Batch 17 for the Academic Session 2025-27 without any legal justification, therefore, some interim relief may be granted in her favour. Accordingly, subject to outcome of this petition, the respondents No.5 to 7 are directed to admit the petitioner in the above course provisionally and allow her to attend the classes by assigning a provisional enrolment number to her. To come up on 07.11.2025. Office is directed to provide copy of order to learned AAG, for compliance. JUDGE JUDGE
